competitors.name: TinyPNG
weaknesses: Limited to 20 images per upload for free users
No advanced settings for compression quality

competitors.name: Squoosh
weaknesses: User interface can be overwhelming for beginners

competitors.name: Compress JPEG & PNG images
features: Supports both JPEG and PNG formats
Option to download all images in a single ZIP file
weaknesses: Limited to 20 images per upload for free users
No advanced settings for compression quality

competitors.name: ImageOptim
weaknesses: Web version is limited compared to the desktop app
No batch processing in the web version

gaps.gap: Advanced compression settings with a user-friendly interface
gaps.implementation: Develop an intuitive UI that allows users to fine-tune compression settings without overwhelming them. Use sliders and presets to simplify the process.
Add basic image editing features such as cropping, resizing, and adjusting brightness/contrast. This can be done using existing open-source libraries like Fabric.js or PixiJS.
Develop a mobile app for iOS and Android that offers the same features as the web version. Ensure it has a simple and intuitive interface suitable for mobile devices.
Allow users to import and export images directly from popular cloud storage services like Google Drive, Dropbox, and OneDrive. Use APIs provided by these services to enable seamless integration.

Use machine learning to analyze images and provide optimal compression settings based on the content and intended use of the image. This can help users achieve the best balance between file size and quality.

recommended_positioning: A user-friendly, feature-rich image compressor with advanced settings and integrated editing tools.
